title: lithocarpus gymnocarpus
intro: Lithocarpus gymnocarpus, also known as the tanbark oak or tan oak, is a deciduous tree native to Asia, belonging to the beech family (Fagaceae). The bark of Lithocarpus gymnocarpus contains tannins, which have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Lithocarpus gymnocarpus have been associated with health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and acting as an antiseptic. Lithocarpus gymnocarpus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its bark is also used in traditional medicine to make various remedies, including salves and ointments.
